channel.title: In That Number
channel.description: Recent content on In That Number

item.title: Teardrops on my Data Frames
item.description: I was browsing Kaggle for an interesting dataset when I ran across a library of Taylor Swift’s songs. OG Taylor, before she went through her “edgy” phase Usually when I find data on Kaggle, it’s been thoroughly picked over. However, luckily for me, only one person had analyzed these data! I thought it might be interesting to look for patterns in Taylor’s music. My running theory for the past decade is that Taylor’s songs all sound the same.

item.title: A dive into comics data
item.description: Comic books have been an important part of my life for the past decade. They have figured differently in my life over time, but nonetheless have maintained a lasting influence over my pop culture choices. In that spirit, my first post will focus on how comic book characters have changed throughout history. In particular, I will focus on how gender and sexuality representation has changed over time. For this analysis, I am using a comics database from 538’s Github page.

item.title: My first post
item.description: I’ve been interested in the field of data journalism for a while now. This blog is an attempt to figure out what intersection of data tinkering and visualization works best for my skillset.

item.title: About me
item.description: Background My name is Allie, and I am a researcher at a think tank in DC. I grew up in New Orleans, hung out in Texas for a minute, and ended up in our nation’s capital. I do miss the deep South, and all the good food/vibes that you just can’t get anywhere else. I have a background in economics. I was enamored by the way that economics imposed order around seemingly inexplicable human behavior.
